factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is expected to increase slightly in 2018 compared to previous years.

evidence.text: We anticipate capital expenditures in 2018 to be approximately $60 to $70 million.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its shareholder return strategy, continuing with cash dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: We used $258.2 million, $245.4 million, and $235.6 million to pay cash dividends in 2017, 2016, and 2015... We also used $185.5 million, $172.9 million, and $229.9 million on share repurchases in 2017, 2016, and 2015.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has been positive and is expected to remain sufficient for the next 12 months.

evidence.text: Assuming no change in our current business plan, management believes that our available cash, together with expected future cash generated from operations, the amount available under our credit facility, and credit available in the market, will be sufficient to satisfy our anticipated needs for working capital, capital expenditures, and cash dividends for at least the next 12 months.

factors.summary: The company has increased its debt leverage with borrowings under credit facilities, but remains in compliance with financial covenants.

evidence.text: The outstanding balance on the revolving credit facility was $715.0 million as of December 31, 2017. During 2017, we had long-term borrowings of $250.0 million on the Receivables Securitization Facility.

factors.summary: Economic recessions pose a significant risk to the company's business.

evidence.text: Economic recessions could have a significant, adverse impact on our business. The transportation industry historically has experienced cyclical fluctuations in financial results due to economic recession, downturns in business cycles of our customers, interest rate fluctuations, and other economic factors beyond our control.

factors.summary: The company may struggle to manage expenses during periods of rapid market change, impacting operating results.

evidence.text: We may not be able to appropriately adjust our expenses to changing market demands. Personnel expenses are our largest expense. In order to maintain high variability in our business model, it is necessary to adjust staffing levels to changing market demands. In periods of rapid change, it is more difficult to match our staffing levels to our business needs.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes could impact the company's operations and costs.

evidence.text: Our operations are subject to the influences of significant political, governmental, and similar changes and our ability to respond to them, including: changes in political conditions and in governmental policies; changes in and compliance with international and domestic laws and regulations; and wars, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations could increase costs and operational challenges.

evidence.text: Our contract carriers are subject to increasingly stringent laws protecting the environment, including those relating to climate change, which could directly or indirectly have a material adverse effect on our business. Future and existing environmental regulatory requirements in the U.S. and abroad could adversely affect operations and increase operating expenses, which in turn could increase our purchased transportation costs.

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors pose risks including conflicts and trade restrictions that could affect the company's operations.

evidence.text: Our operations are subject to the influences of significant political, governmental, and similar changes and our ability to respond to them, including: changes in political conditions and in governmental policies; changes in and compliance with international and domestic laws and regulations; and wars, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts.

factors.summary: The company's long-term growth objective is to grow earnings per share by 10 percent, but there are no specific forward guidance details provided in the text.

evidence.text: Our long-term growth objective is to grow earnings per share by 10 percent. Long-term growth targets represent an over time perspective and do not necessarily represent an expected annual growth rate. There can be no assurance that our long-term growth objective will be achieved or that we will be able to effectively adapt our management, administrative, and operational systems to respond to any future growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include margin compression due to increased costs and pricing pressures, particularly in truckload services.

evidence.text: Truckload margin compression was a challenge to our earnings per share during much of the year. In 2017, changing market conditions continued to impact our results. We had volume increases in all of our service lines, and experienced pricing and cost increases in nearly all of our service lines, which negatively impacted our margins.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and transportation costs are significant factors impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: This decrease in net revenue margin was driven by increases in transportation costs, including fuel. Fuel prices declined throughout 2015, which contributed to slower growth of our total revenues and an increase in our transportation net revenue margins.

factors.summary: The company has faced challenges in maintaining pricing power, as transportation costs have risen faster than customer pricing, leading to margin compression and decreased net revenue margins.

evidence.text: Higher carrier prices may result in decreased net revenue margin. Carriers can be expected to charge higher prices if market conditions warrant, or to cover higher operating expenses. Our net revenues and income from operations may decrease if we are unable to increase our pricing to our customers.

NAST net revenue margin decreased primarily due to transportation costs growing faster than customer pricing in 2017 compared to 2016. Global Forwarding net revenue margin decreased due to transportation costs increasing at a faster rate than customer pricing.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Truckload margin compression was a challenge to our earnings per share during much of the year. In 2017, changing market conditions continued to impact our results. We had volume increases in all of our service lines, and experienced pricing and cost increases in nearly all of our service lines, which negatively impacted our margins.

factors.summary: Overall, volume trends are positive, with increases across multiple segments contributing to revenue growth. However, there is a risk that economic downturns could negatively impact freight volumes, potentially limiting growth opportunities.

evidence.text: The increases in accounts receivable and accounts payable are primarily the result of increased volumes.

A reduction in overall freight volumes in the marketplace reduces our opportunities for growth. A significant portion of our freight is transactional or 'spot' market opportunities. The transactional market may be more impacted than the freight market by overall economic conditions.

NAST truckload volumes increased approximately 4.5 percent in 2017 compared to 2016. NAST LTL volumes increased approximately eight percent in 2017 compared to 2016. Global Forwarding volumes increased across all services in 2017.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues increased 15.4 percent to $13.5 billion in 2017 from $11.7 billion in 2016. This increase in transportation revenues was driven by volume increases in all of our transportation services and increased customer pricing in most services.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in transportation and fuel, which are putting pressure on margins. Fuel price fluctuations and regulatory changes are significant factors impacting net revenue margins, with evidence showing increases in transportation costs and related services.

evidence.text: Changing fuel costs and interruptions of fuel supplies may have an impact on our net revenue margins. In our truckload transportation business, which is the largest source of our net revenues, fluctuating fuel prices may result in decreased net revenue margin. While our different pricing arrangements with customers and contracted carriers make it very difficult to measure the precise impact, we believe that fuel costs essentially act as a pass-through cost to our truckload business. In times of fluctuating fuel prices, our net revenue margin may also fluctuate.

NAST cost of purchased transportation and related services increased 13.7 percent to $8.2 billion in 2017 from $7.2 billion in 2016. Global Forwarding costs of transportation and related services increased 40.7 percent to $1.7 billion in 2017 from $1.2 billion in 2016.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: This decrease in net revenue margin was driven by increases in transportation costs, including fuel. Fuel prices declined throughout 2015, which contributed to slower growth of our total revenues and an increase in our transportation net revenue margins.

factors.summary: Labor costs are a significant and increasing expense, driven by higher headcount and incentive plans, which are putting pressure on margins. Effective management is crucial to mitigate potential margin strain.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses are our largest expense. In order to maintain high variability in our business model, it is necessary to adjust staffing levels to changing market demands. In periods of rapid change, it is more difficult to match our staffing levels to our business needs.

NAST operating expenses increased 5.5 percent in 2017 to $897.0 million from $849.9 million in 2016. This was due to an increase in personnel expenses related to incentive plans that are designed to keep expenses variable with changes in net revenues and profitability, and an increase in selling, general, and administrative expenses.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Personnel expenses increased 10.8 percent to $1.2 billion in 2017 from $1.1 billion in 2016. Personnel expenses as a percentage of net revenue increased in 2017 to 49.8 percent from 46.8 percent in 2016. The increase in personnel expense was due primarily to growth in our average headcount of 7.4 percent in 2017 compared to 2016 and increases in expenses related to incentive plans that are designed to keep expenses variable with changes in net revenues and profitability.

factors.summary: The revenue mix shifted towards lower-margin segments in 2017, negatively impacting margins. This shift was particularly evident in sourcing services, where net revenue decreased due to lower market pricing and changes in service mix.

evidence.text: NAST net revenue margin decreased primarily due to transportation costs growing faster than customer pricing in 2017 compared to 2016. Global Forwarding net revenue margin decreased due to transportation costs increasing at a faster rate than customer pricing.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Total sourcing revenues decreased 5.1 percent to $1.37 billion in 2017 from $1.44 billion in 2016. Purchased products sourced for resale decreased 5.5 percent in 2017 to $1.2 billion from $1.3 billion in 2016. These decreases were primarily due to lower market pricing and change in service mix.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments. While overall transportation revenues increased, driven by volume and pricing hikes, specific segments like NAST and Global Forwarding saw significant revenue growth, indicating strong demand in those areas. However, other segments might have faced challenges, leading to mixed demand trends.

evidence.text: NAST revenues increased 11.3 percent to $9.7 billion in 2017 compared to $8.7 billion in 2016. This was primarily due to increased volumes and increased pricing to our customers.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Global Forwarding revenues increased 36.0 percent to $2.1 billion in 2017 from $1.6 billion in 2016. This increase was primarily related to increased volumes in all services and increased customer pricing.

Total transportation revenues increased 15.4 percent to $13.5 billion in 2017 from $11.7 billion in 2016. This increase in transportation revenues was driven by volume increases in all of our transportation services and increased customer pricing in most services.

factors.summary: The company experienced mixed organic growth in 2017 compared to 2016. While certain segments, like Global Forwarding, showed strong revenue increases, there was a deceleration in overall organic revenue growth. This was partly due to a slowdown in net revenue growth despite increases in transportation volumes, with margins decreasing due to higher costs, including fuel.

evidence.text: Global Forwarding revenues increased 36.0 percent to $2.1 billion in 2017 from $1.6 billion in 2016. This increase was primarily related to increased volumes in all services and increased customer pricing.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Total transportation net revenues increased 4.2 percent in 2017 to $2.25 billion from $2.15 billion in 2016. Our transportation net revenue margin decreased to 16.6 percent in 2017 from 18.4 percent in 2016. This decrease in net revenue margin was driven by increases in transportation costs, including fuel.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure, with both gross and operating margins compressing due to rising costs outpacing revenue growth and increased pricing pressures.

evidence.text: NAST net revenue margin decreased primarily due to transportation costs growing faster than customer pricing in 2017 compared to 2016.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Global Forwarding net revenue margin decreased due to transportation costs increasing at a faster rate than customer pricing.
Income from operations decreased 7.5 percent to $775.1 million in 2017 from $837.5 million in 2016.
Income from operations as a percentage of net revenues decreased to 32.7 percent in 2017 from 36.8 percent in 2016.

factors.summary: The company's end market momentum shows mixed performance across different segments. While the Global Forwarding and NAST segments demonstrated strong growth, with Global Forwarding revenues increasing by 36.0% and NAST showing increased volumes and customer pricing, the Robinson Fresh segment faced challenges with a 3.7% decrease in net revenues. Additionally, the sourcing segment experienced a slowdown, with total sourcing revenues decreasing by 5.1% and purchased products sourced for resale declining by 5.5% due to lower market pricing and a shift in service mix.

evidence.text: Global Forwarding revenues increased 36.0 percent to $2.1 billion in 2017 from $1.6 billion in 2016. This increase was primarily related to increased volumes in all services and increased customer pricing.
evidence.subsection: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS- 2017 COMPARED TO 2016

evidence.text: Robinson Fresh net revenues decreased 3.7 percent to $226.1 million in 2017 from $234.8 million in 2016. This decrease was primarily due to declines in transportation net revenues.

Total sourcing revenues decreased 5.1 percent to $1.37 billion in 2017 from $1.44 billion in 2016. Purchased products sourced for resale decreased 5.5 percent in 2017 to $1.2 billion from $1.3 billion in 2016. These decreases were primarily due to lower market pricing and change in service mix.
